title: ‘The Invite’ Is a Hilarious Social Nightmare, With Olivia Wilde!
format: The whole episode is about 'The Invite,' Olivia Wilde's one-set dinner-party dramedy, plus a roundtable of favorite toxic-couple movies (five each) and an interview with Wilde on shooting in sequence, the Edward Norton monologue, and the ambiguous ending.

segments.heading: 'The Invite'
segments.note: Sean, Amanda, and Chris Ryan on the whole movie

films.title: The Invite
films.note: Wilde directs and stars; Rashida Jones and Will McCormick wrote it, adapted from a 2021 Spanish film. Seth Rogen, Edward Norton, and Penelope Cruz round out the four-hander, playing two couples at one dinner party that turns into a group-sex proposition and a marriage on the rocks. Shot on 35mm, in sequence, on a single built set. Dedicated to Diane Keaton; the hosts call it Wilde's best work and read the piano-bookend ending as a Rorschach test.

segments.heading: Toxic-couple canon
segments.note: five each: the nightmare-marriage movies they love

picks.title: Unfaithful
picks.note: Richard Gere and Diane Lane; everything looks great until a windy day in Soho.

picks.title: The Lion in Winter
picks.note: Peter O'Toole and Katharine Hepburn saying incredibly rude things: 'the 15th-century Husbands and Wives.'

picks.title: Reality Bites
picks.note: Lelaina and Troy are insufferable, but the movie's logic demands they end up together.

picks.title: Midsommar
picks.note: Florence Pugh and Jack Reynor; she stays much longer than she should.

picks.title: Phantom Thread
picks.note: Her aspirational pick: Reynolds Woodcock and Alma as a portrait of marriage, timely after her husband's shoulder surgery.

picks.title: Rope
picks.note: Brandon and Phillip, two men who love a dinner party and want to kill people. Hitchcock.

picks.title: The Dark Knight Rises
picks.note: Bruce Wayne and Talia al Ghul; she activates a new side of him, then betrays him.

picks.title: Natural Born Killers
picks.note: Mickey and Mallory, played by Woody Harrelson and Juliette Lewis.

picks.title: Casino
picks.note: Ace and Ginger, two people who should not have been in a relationship.

picks.title: Gone Girl
picks.note: Nick and Amy: likely his No. 1, and very toxic.

picks.title: The One I Love
picks.note: Mark Duplass and Elisabeth Moss; also opens on a couple at a piano, then twists into what you wish your partner could be.

picks.title: Husbands and Wives
picks.note: Sally and Jack, Judy Davis and Sydney Pollack; the Pollack performance for Sean, especially once Davis beds Liam Neeson.

picks.title: Woman in the Dunes
picks.note: Teshigahara, a man trapped in a dune with a woman; 'the Invite prequel.'

picks.title: Deathtrap
picks.note: Sidney Lumet; Michael Caine and Christopher Reeve as mentor-mentee rivals plotting something else.

picks.title: The Lighthouse
picks.note: Pattinson and Dafoe, two keepers, both named Thomas, who might be the same person. 'Sometimes marriages can feel like that.'

films.title: The Invite
films.note: The cast asked her to play Angela after workshopping the material; shooting in sequence let her feel the film's rhythm in her body. Norton wrote his own third-act monologue and asked to perform it first on camera aimed at her. Dev Hines scored it; test-screening notes killed a set of flashbacks so she'd 'stick the landing.' She reads the ending as a hopeful breakup; Rogen thinks the couple stays together forever.

films.title: Erupcja
films.note: Her last-great-thing pick: Pete Ohs's film with Charli xcx, shot on a Canon 5D in about nine days, about a woman who wants love to feel like a volcano.

films: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant Mayhem

Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant May.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant Mayhem
